Hotel Royal Sonesta

Royal Sonesta Penthouse Terrace (39th floor) 
Sign in at the registration table to receive your badge and welcome 
materials. 
 1:00 p.m. - 1:30 p.m. 
Welcome to ACE Illinois 
Royal Sonesta Penthouse Terrace (39th floor)
 
Join us in the Royal Sonesta Penthouse to welcome you to the 20th 
Americas Competitiveness Exchange
 Margo Markopolous, Deputy Director for Trade and Investment, 
Illinois Department of Commerce and Economic Opportunity
 Jeannette Tamayo, Associate Vice President for Workforce
Development and Community Engagement/Executive Director, 
University of Illinois System
 Cesar Parga, Section Chief Section of Competitiveness, Innovation 
and Technology Department of Economic Development Executive 
Secretariat for Integral Development, 
Organization of American States
 1:30 p.m. Presentation: “Promoting Rural 
Community and Economic Development through 
Public Sector Partnerships”
This presentation explores how Western Illinois University (WIU) – a 
rural, regional public university – promotes rural community and 
economic development. WIU offers an array of services, including 
community planning, entrepreneurship, rural internships, and 
cooperative development. However, it succeeds to the extent that 
it has strong relationships with local, state, and federal government 
agencies. Hence, this presentation will focus on strategies WIU 
adopts to connect the network of government agencies with rural 
communities. To provide some context, the presentation will also 
briefly discuss how WIU worked with Universidad Tecnologica de la 
Selva (UTS) a rural, regional public university in Ocosingo, Chiapas, 
Mexico. The relationship between WIU and UTS is a model that can 
be replicated to benefit rural and regional universities in the United 
States and in the countries of the Americas.
 Chris Merrett, Western Illinois University
 Winthrop Phippen, Western Illinois University
 2:30 p.m. Break
 2:45 p.m. Presentation: “Human Centered-Design 
and Social Innovation / Entrepreneurship” 
Human-Centered Design (HCD) is a problem-solving approach that 
uses design thinking methods and tools to identify the unmet needs 
of a population to collaboratively and iteratively develop solutions. 
Learn how to leverage this discipline’s skill set in a 90-minute 
intensive led by the Director of the Siebel Center for Design at the 
University of Illinois Urbana-Champaign. 
 Rachel Switzky, Siebel Center for Design, University of Illinois 
Urbana-Champaign
4:20 p.m. 
 Gather for bus departures
4:30 p.m. 
 
Transfer to 
 Navy Pier 
 
Historic Navy Pier is an iconic thread in the fabric of Chicago. 
As the world’s largest public pier, it is a top-attended leisure 
destination in the Midwest, attracting more than 9 million 
guests a year. When they arrive, guests discover an exciting, 
ever-evolving “People’s Pier” for the 21st century, featuring 
an array of arts, cultural, educational, entertainment, and 
special events programming that offers a uniquely Chicago 
experience. In honor of its 100th anniversary, Navy Pier 
unveiled dramatically reimagined, pier-wide enhancements, 
new dining options, and compelling landscape and design 
features that continue to elevate the Pier’s status.
 5:00 p.m. Walk to Group Photo 
Spot on Navy Pier 
 5:30 p.m. Explore Navy Pier in small, guided tour groups 
led by a Navy Pier team member and discover the history and 
transformation of this iconic landmark into the Midwest’s premier 
nonprofit tourism destination.
7:45 p.m. 
H
 
 Transfer to Hotel

Illinois

8:00 a.m. 
 Welcome Breakfast with Local Leadership
 
Convened by the Organization of American States (OAS) and the 
United States Government, through the Department of Commerce 
(Economic Development Administration) and the Department of State 
(Permanent Mission to the OAS). ACE Illinois is presented by the Illinois 
Department of Commerce and Economic Opportunity (DCEO), the 
Greater Chicagoland Economic Partnership (GCEP), Intersect Illinois, 
Illinois Innovation Network, World Business Chicago, and the University 
of Illinois System.
Hear from key partners as they introduce you to their respective 
organizations and share information on the motivation and goals of ACE 
Illinois.
 Official Opening Ceremony
 Welcome Remarks and introductions

Economic Opportunity

Innovation, University of Illinois System

Organization of American States

Transfer to 
 UIC Innovation Center
 
The UIC Innovation Center is a space for interdisciplinary innovation and 
education, reflecting its dynamic mission through its frequent changes 
to meet the needs of projects and classes. The Innovation Center 
gives students a chance to learn through experience with continuous 
collaboration, learning, making, and remaking. Corporate and university 
partners engage with the center to generate human-centered solutions 
focused on deep research and exploratory development.
 10:00 a.m. Spark Talks: How Innovation Powers 
Health Progress
 
The UIC Innovation Center is proud to highlight celebrated researchers 
and program directors and to share examples of and discuss current 
topics related to health innovation. In addition, key partners in the UI 
Health system will join us to share real-world examples of how health 
innovation is changing the lives of community members.
 11:00 a.m. Interactive Tours of the UIC Innovation 
Center
 11:30 a.m. Group Photo 
11:35 a.m.

 MxD
 
Manufacturing x Digital advances economic prosperity and national 
security by strengthening U.S. manufacturing competitiveness through 
technology innovation, workforce development, and cybersecurity 
preparedness. In partnership with the Department of Defense, MxD 
convene an ecosystem to solve critical manufacturing challenges. 
MxD is also the National Center for Cybersecurity in Manufacturing as 
designated by DoD.

MxD leadership 
 Bernadino Barratta, CEO, MxD 
 Jela Trask, Vice President, Membership and External Relations

Attendees will tour the facility to experience demonstrations at the 
Discrete Testbed (a manufacturing line equipped with sensors for 
data collection), the Cyber Wall (a simulation of cyber-attacks on 
factories), the Wireless Demonstration Testbed, and augmented 
reality training programs.

 1871
1871 is a private collective of trailblazing founders, visionary leaders, resilient 
innovators, and steadfast supporters in tech. Immerse in our programs, 
resources, and community uniquely designed for growing a vibrant tech 
ecosystem.
 3:15 p.m. Welcome from Betsy Ziegler (CEO, 1871) 
and Jaime Di Paulo - Illinois Hispanic Chamber of 
Commerce - IHCC
IHCC stands as a dynamic and influential force, empowering over 
15,000 Hispanic entrepreneurs and businesses throughout Illinois. 
 3:45 p.m. Interactive Panel “Chambers of Commerce 
as Community Connectors”
Learn how the IHCC is able to connect Hispanic businesses to non-
Latino markets, and connect non-Hispanic businesses to Latino 
markets. As the largest Midwest Hispanic Chamber of Commerce, 
IHCC is a crucial connector in the community and business sectors. 
Alongside remarks from IHCC representatives, attendees will hear 
directly from an IHCC member who has directly benefited by their 
services and incorporated new strategies for growth. 
 4:35 p.m. Group Photo 
4:40 p.m. 
 Walk to 
 MATTER within Merchandise Mart
 
MATTER supports healthcare startups at all stages of growth by 
creating access to learning and development opportunities, capital and 
customers, and a dynamic community. From Fortune 500 companies 
to non-profit health systems and academic institutions, we empower 
industry-leading organizations to source and co-develop solutions, 
build innovation capacity and solve complex problems. 
 4:45 p.m. Welcome and Overview: “Meet Matter” by 
Matter CEO Steven Collens (TBC)

 1871

Transfer to
 University of IL Research Park 
The University of Illinois Urbana-Champaign: Research Park is an innovation 
hub for technology commercialization with a unique environment that 
cultivates startups and increases capabilities for established companies. 
Located on campus, Research Park has more than 120 companies and 
growing, employing over 2,100 people in high-technology careers.
 10:30 a.m. Welcome and Round Robin Tours
 Dr. Beth Conerty, Associate Director of Business Development 
IBRL, Regional Innovation Officer iFAB Tech Hub
 Integrated Bioprocessing Research Lab - IBRL
The IBTL, is a pilot-scale bioprocessing product research and development facility 
that bridges the gap from basic discovery to commercialization of bioproducts. 
The facility serves as an invaluable asset to the bioenergy and bioprocessing 
companies by partnering them with the human and equipment capabilities of 
the university.
 Food Science and Human Nutrition Pilot Processing 
Plant
The Food Science pilot plant, is a newly renovated modern, flexible facility built 
to meet the needs of students, researchers, and the international food industry. 
Plug-and-play utilities, large equipment selection, and a product development 
kitchen provide an ideal environment for hands-on learning opportunities, new 
product or process development, and small-scale food production.
 iFAB Tech Hub
In 2024, iFAB was awarded just over $51M by the U.S. Economic Development 
Administration and designated as one of 31 inaugural Regional Innovation 
and Technology Hubs. The iFAB consortium, led by the University of Illinois’ 
Integrated Bioprocessing Research Laboratory (IBRL), comprises over 30 
partners from academia, industry, government, and nonprofits from across 
Illinois. iFAB drives growth in the precision fermentation and bioprocessing 
industry by linking assets, addressing gaps, and connecting infrastructure with 
wraparound programming to transform the region into a globally competitive 
precision fermentation lab-to-line ecosystem. 
 11:45 a.m. Group Photo

Center, Illinois Ballroom “University-Driven 
Innovation and Entrepreneurship”
 
 Kim Kidwell, Associate Chancellor for Strategic Partnerships and 
Initiatives (moderator)

 Mike Tai, Co-Founder, Boston Bioprocess

 Tour of the Center for Advanced Bioenergy and

and discussion of NCSA’s work in AI and quantum
Santiago Nunez-Corrales, Quantum Lead Senior Research Scientist, National

 2:00 p.m. Presentation: “Illinois’ Rapidly Growing 
Quantum Ecosystem”
 
Quantum technologies have the potential to tackle many of the 
biggest challenges in healthcare, energy, transportation, financial 
services, agriculture and national security. Illinois has quickly become 
a leader in the emerging quantum computing industry thanks 
largely to University research efforts and state investments including 
the recently announced Illinois Quantum and Microelectronics Park 
(IQMP). 
 
Welcome remarks: 
 
 Dr. Susan A. Martinis, University of Illinois Urbana-Champaign 
Vice Chancellor for Research and Innovation
 
 Harley Johnson, Chief Executive Office and Director, Illinois 
Quantum and Microelectronics Park
 
 Brian DeMarco, Chief Technology Officer and Director, Illinois 
Quantum and Microelectronics Park

Quantum Communication Links”

 Distillery Labs
Distillery Labs is a proud member of the Illinois Innovation Network and 
serves as the region’s premier innovation hub in downtown Peoria, Illinois. 
We support entrepreneurs and startups by providing space, mentorship, 
and connections to capital, talent, and industry partners. Our mission is to 
inspire, connect, educate, and motivate the next generation of innovators.

The Innovation Exchange Coalition (IEC) supports global innovation 
and entrepreneurship by providing mentorship, market access, and 
technical expertise to tech entrepreneurs seeking to expand their 
networks, scale manufacturing, and accelerate commercialization. 
During this session, eight companies will deliver rapid-fire 
presentations showcasing their solutions and exploring potential 
partnerships.

 Breakfast & Panel at Royal Sonesta 
Penthouse with Winton & Strawn
Winston & Strawn LLP is an international law firm headquartered in 
Chicago. It has more than 975 attorneys spread across ten offices in the 
United States and five offices in Europe, Asia, and South America. 
 Talbert Navia, Partner, Chair, Latin America & Caribbean

Transfer to
 The Chicago Metropolitan 
Agency for Planning - CMAP
The CMAP is a regional metropolitan planning organization that works on 
behalf of the 7 counties, 284 municipalities, and 8.6 million residents of 
northeastern Illinois. Through planning, policy research and analysis, and 
data services, CMAP supports economic growth, prioritized investments, 
and regional resilience. 
 9:30 a.m. Panel: “Transportation and Economic 
Growth”
 
CMAP staff will provide an overview of the agency’s transportation 
planning and policy work and how these initiatives support 
economic development in the northeastern Illinois region. Staff 
will discuss the agency’s long-range planning objectives, policy 
research and development work, and technical assistance offerings 
to communities. 
 10:30 a.m. Panel: “Regional Economic 
Competitiveness”
 
The Greater Chicagoland Economic Partnership (GCEP) is a united 
strategy facilitated by the seven counties of northeastern Illinois 
(Cook, DuPage, Kane, Kendall, Lake, McHenry, Will) and the City of 
Chicago to work together to drive economic development. GCEP 
represents the region’s combined assets, including its extensive 
freight infrastructure, talent, strong exporting industries, and world-
class institutions of innovation, research, and culture. 
 11:15 a.m. Guided Tours: Old Chicago Post Office 
and Group Photo 
 
The Old Chicago Post Office was originally completed in 1921, but 
underwent a monumental expansion in 1932 to meet Chicago’s 
unprecedented postal needs. The meteoric rise of the mail-order 
industry, spearheaded by the Chicago-based Montgomery Ward 
and Sears catalogs, transformed the city into the distribution center 
of the nation nearly overnight. In its postal heyday, the property 
was the largest of its kind in the world, capable of shuttling an 
astonishing 19 million pieces of mail daily. Recent renovations have 
shifted the post office to a mixed-use building.
11:50 a.m. 
 Gather for bus departures 
12:00 p.m. 
Transfer to
 Daley College
Located on the southwest side of Chicago, Daley College serves 
approximately 12,000 students annually across our two campuses 
through 
credit, 
Adult 
Education, 
and 
Continuing 
Education 
programming. 
 1:00 p.m. Lunch at Richard J. Daley College & 
Make It In Illinois Campaign 
 
Make It In Illinois is a statewide digital marketing and grassroots 
engagement campaign aimed at growing the manufacturing 
workforce talent pool and increasing enrollment in training 
programs. The Illinois Department of Commerce and Economic 
Opportunity and the Illinois Innovation Network partner on this 
effort with Richard J. Daley College to position manufacturing as a 
lifelong, family-supporting career pathway open to all. 
 2:30 p.m. Welcome and Presentation 
“Serving Students and Community Organization 
Partnerships” by Dr. Janine Janosky (President of 
Richard J. Daley College) 
 3:00 p.m. Tour of the new Manufacturing 
Technology and Engineering Center - MTEC
 
This new facility spans 52,000 sf and is fully equipped with a 
high-bay space and ten engineering and manufacturing labs 
that provide students with hands-on training on professional 
equipment for highly specialized, technology-oriented careers in 
manufacturing.
 3:50 p.m. Group Photo 
 4:00 p.m. Local Community to Global 
Connections: “How local community development 
organizations in Chicago can assist in developing 
international relationships” by Jim Longino of the 
Greater Southwest Development Corporation 
 
(Beatty Theater)
 
The Greater Southwest Development Corporation works to create, 
attract, retain, and expand commercial and manufacturing 
businesses on Chicago’s Southwest Side and is uniquely positioned 
to assist businesses through a variety of development services that 
include access to capital, business and marketing plan assistance, 
international trade, procurement, certifications, and licensing. 
5:00 p.m. 
 Gather for bus departures
5:15 p.m. 
 
Transfer to
 The Hatchery
The Hatchery Chicago is a non-profit food and beverage incubator 
dedicated to helping local entrepreneurs build and grow successful 
businesses. Through cutting edge commercial kitchen facilities, 
robust entrepreneurship curriculums, and partnership with generous 
corporate sponsors and foundations, The Hatchery supports local food 
and beverage entrepreneurs, cultivates local job opportunities, and 
accelerates local economic growth. 
 5:45 p.m. Presentation and Tour
Join rotating tours of The Hatchery’s new incubator space. KOVAL 
(A Chicago-based organic spirits distillery) will provide tastings 
from their line of distinct spirits. Garrett’s Popcorn will provide their 
handcrafted gourmet popcorn made in Chicago. World Business 
Chicago will provide an assortment of Illinois beers and beverages. 
 Jason and Erica London, The Chef and the Baker
 Brad McConnell, Allies for Community Business
 Mary Fran Riley, Allies for Community Business
 6:50 p.m. Group Photo 
 7:00 p.m. A Taste of Chicago with World Business 
Chicago 
Includes attendance from Hatchery leadership and participating 
businesses. World Business Chicago will provide additional remarks.

Transfer to
 Will County Inland Port
 10:30 a.m. Panel / Presentation(s): “America 
Largest Inland Port Bus Tour”
The inland port in Will County, Illinois—centered in Joliet and 
Elwood—is one of North America’s largest intermodal logistics hubs. 
Spanning 6,000 acres and developed by CenterPoint Properties, 
it combines rail, road, and warehouse infrastructure to efficiently 
move goods throughout the Midwest and beyond. Anchored by 
BNSF and Union Pacific rail terminals, it handles international 
shipping containers arriving from coast to coast. These containers are 
quickly transferred to massive adjacent distribution centers serving 
companies like Amazon, Walmart, and many more. The port’s layout 
minimizes trucking distances, reduces congestion, and supports a 
growing logistics workforce. A tour showcases this high-efficiency 
industrial landscape, including sprawling warehouses, cranes, and 
active rail operations.
 Doug Pryor, President & CEO, Will County Center for Economic 
Development
 John Greuling, Founder, JEG 360, LLC
 11:30 a.m. Panel / Presentation(s): “Why Will County 
Works? Regional Assets Driving Business Success”
 This presentation will explore the key assets that make Will County 
an ideal location for business attraction and growth. Attendees 
will gain insight into the region’s strategic advantages, including 
its central location, strong infrastructure, diverse workforce, and 
vibrant industry base. Hosted at Joliet Junior College, the session will 
highlight how regional collaboration and investment continue to 
position Will County as a leader in economic development.
 Doug Pryor, President & CEO, Will County Center for Economic 
Development
 John Greuling, Founder, JEG 360, LLC
12:10 p.m. 
 Gather for bus departures

Transfer to
 Ecolab
Ecolab is a global sustainability leader offering water, hygiene, and 
infection prevention solutions and services that protect people and 
resources. Customers in more than 40 industries choose Ecolab’s 
comprehensive science-based solutions, data-driven insights, and 
world-class service to advance food safety, maintain clean and safe 
environments, and optimize water conservation and energy use. 
 2:00 p.m. Presentation and Tour: Water University
A unique, high-tech learning environment, Water University is 
dedicated to developing expertise of customers and associates to 
minimize water usage, maximize results, and optimize total cost of 
operations. Our facility is home to more than 200 researchers assisting 
customers from small businesses to Fortune 500 companies solve 
water challenges and improve their bottom line. 
 3:15 p.m. Group Photo 
3:20 p.m. 
 Gather for bus departures
3:25 p.m. 
 
Transfer to
 Naper Settlement
 
The Birck Family Innovation Gateway is the new “front door” to Naper 
Settlement, welcoming guests with an engaging introduction to 
Naperville’s 20th- and 21st-century history. This dynamic space will 
feature a digital exhibit where visitors can explore state and national 
narratives alongside local stories—and even contribute their own 
photos, files, and memories. 
 3:35 p.m. Presentation: “Understanding the 
Naperville Riverwalk”
Learn more about the decades of work the city and community have 
undergone to make the Riverwalk a thriving destination. 
 Mayor Scott A. Wehrli, Mayor, City of Naperville 
 Bill Novack, Director of Transportation, Engineering, and 
Development, City of Naperville
 5:00 p.m. Group Photo 
 5:10 p.m. Networking Reception: The Birck Family 
Innovation Gateway
Considered the “Crown Jewel” of Naperville, the Riverwalk is a place 
of beauty and tranquility along the West Branch of the DuPage River. 
Created in 1981 to honor Naperville’s 150th anniversary, the Riverwalk 
features 1.75 miles of brick paths, fountains, bridges, meeting and 
event spaces, outdoor sculpture and artwork, recreation facilities, and 
celebratory memorials.

 Gather for bus departures

In the spirit of international cooperation. Consul General of Canada 
in Chicago Madeleine Féquière welcomes the ACE delegation for a

9:25 a.m. 
 Gather for bus departures
9:30 a.m. 
 
Transfer to
 Discovery Partners Institute
 
​The Discovery Partners Institute (DPI), an initiative of the University of Illinois 
System, is a Chicago-based hub that cultivates tech talent, conducts 
applied research, and fosters business development to strengthen the 
state’s innovation economy. DPI was created to train people for high-
demand tech jobs, conduct applied research and development, and 
build businesses, and that vital work continues. It was also designed to be 
nimble and move quickly to serve the people of Illinois. 
 10:10 a.m. Welcome and Overview from Discovery 
Partners Institute
Learn more about how DPI’s collaborative approach—with universities, industry, 
and government—positions Illinois as a national leader in innovation and workforce 
transformation.
 Venkat Venkatakrishnan, Director of Research, Discovery Partners Institute
 10:20 a.m. Presentation: “The Value of Data in 
Evidence-Based Policy Making” 
EDA has funded an interdisciplinary economic research center at Argonne 
National Laboratory (Argonne), The National Economic Research and Resilience 
Center (NERRC). Hear from NERCC Director about how they provide innovative 
and transparent economic data access, research, and analysis to support and 
strengthen economic development and resilience in communities across the 
nation.
 Iain Hyde, Director, National Economic Research & Resilience Center, Argonne 
National Laboratory
 11:00 a.m. Presentation: “Comprehensive Economic 
Development Strategy (CEDS)” 
CEDS contributes to effective economic development in America’s communities 
and regions through a place-based, regionally driven economic development 
planning process. Economic development planning – as implemented through the 
CEDS – is not only a cornerstone of EDA programs, but successfully serves as a means 
to engage community leaders, leverage the involvement of the private sector, and 
establish a strategic blueprint for regional collaboration. 
 Kevin Lindenman, Illinois Association of Regional Councils and North Central 
Illinois Council of Governments
 11:20 a.m. Group Photo 
 
11:25 a.m. 
 Gather for bus departures
11:30 a.m. 
 
Transfer to 
 mHUB
 
mHUB is an innovation center for all things HardTech. Through prototyping 
labs, industry partnerships, and a commitment to U.S. manufacturing, 
the mHUB ecosystem is designed to remove barriers and increase the 
probability of building a sustainable business to commercialize new 
physical products. 
 12:00 p.m. Welcome and Panel: “Building the 
HardTech Ecosystem: Partners and Players” 
The Fourth Industrial Revolution is driven by the intersection of digital 
and hardware innovation, and Chicago leads R&D breakthroughs 
in advanced technologies including batteries, quantum and edge 
computing, electronics and sensors, cybersecurity, nanotechnology, 
AGtech, and MedTech. 
 1:00 p.m. Lunch
 2:00 p.m. Guided Tours of mHUB + Quantum Freezer 
Attendees will tour the new 80,000 sf innovation center led by mHUB 
leadership. This new facility on the city’s near West Side opened in 2023 
and helps anchor the revitalization of a neighborhood immediately 
outside of downtown Chicago, bringing opportunity and job creation 
to this Chicago community. 
 2:40 p.m. Group Photo 
 2:45 p.m. Presentation: “Hard Tech Startups“
 
mHUB leadership will moderate a group conversation on implementing 
HardTech solutions in different settings. Learn how to access mHUB 
facilities, partnerships, and ecosystem to augment and support your 
own ecosystem development, gain insights on best practices, and 
failsafe strategies.
